steps.title: Relate the graph to f
steps.content: The graph shows $y=f(x)+2$. So $f(x)=\bigl(f(x)+2\bigr)-2$ — subtract $2$ from every y-value on the graph to recover $f$.

steps.title: Identify f from the y-intercept
steps.content:
The displayed graph has y-intercept $4$, so $f(0)+2=4$ and $f(0)=2$. Test $f(x)=-6^{x}+3$: $f(0)=-1+3=2$. The decreasing exponential shape matches as well, so $f(x)=-6^{x}+3$.

That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
